howToRender.summary: Everything needed to redraw this chart and re-align "drawing" with the stars, independent of this app.
howToRender.starProjection: Place star (ra,dec) at gnomonic(ra, dec, view.centerRa, view.centerDec) -> {x,y} tangent-plane units, then apply your own pan/zoom/scale to pixels.
howToRender.gnomonicFormula: With ra0=view.centerRa, dec0=view.centerDec (radians): c = sin(dec0)*sin(dec) + cos(dec0)*cos(dec)*cos(ra-ra0); x = cos(dec)*sin(ra-ra0)/c; y = (cos(dec0)*sin(dec) - sin(dec0)*cos(dec)*cos(ra-ra0))/c.
howToRender.similarityTransform: projection.fit maps a tangent-plane point g (centered on projection.tangentRa/tangentDec) to ORIGINAL drawing-image pixels: x = fit.sRe*g.x - fit.sIm*g.y + fit.tx; y = fit.sIm*g.x + fit.sRe*g.y + fit.ty. Invert it (complex division) to go pixel -> tangent-plane.
howToRender.drawingOverlay: The "drawing" image is in ORIGINAL pixel space. For each source pixel (x,y): 1) target={x, y:-y}; 2) g = inverse(target, projection.fit); 3) (ra,dec) = inverseGnomonic(g, projection.tangentRa, projection.tangentDec); 4) reproject via gnomonic(ra,dec, view.centerRa, view.centerDec) to land it in the same tangent-plane units as the stars. This map is curved (not affine) — warp via a grid of small triangles (e.g. 20x20), each locally affine-fit from its own 3 corners, not one transform for the whole image.

observerDefault.label: Edit to your own location — see obsLat/obsLon in the app
